LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER SPATARO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT WAS A BONA-FIDE ENCUMBRANCES. E-MAILS INDICATE AMY GREEN, DEFENDANT'S ESCROW AGENT, KNEW ABOUT PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF'S POSSIBLE INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY, AND THE PRIOR LITIGATION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND RANSOM. JUDGMENT ON SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT WEBSITE INDICATED RANSOM NO LONGER HAD AN INTEREST IN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED REGARDING DEFENDANT'S IMPUTED KNOWLEDGE FROM HIS ESCROW AGENT. (SEE TRIPLE A MANAGEMENT CO., INC. V. FRISONE (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 520.) SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED DOMINION OVER HER EQUITY "DAMAGED IN AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO THE VALUE OF THE EQUITY APPROPRIATED," NOT TITLE, SO IT IS A CLAIM FOR PERSONAL PROPERTY. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED REGARDING INJUNCTION CAUSE OF ACTION WHERE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS SUPPORT AN INJUNCTION. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT RISE TO MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) ORD. RE: OBJEC. TO PLFT'S EVID., ETC. FILED IN OPEN CRT. AND COPIES GIVEN TO COUNSEL AT HEARING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2011 Off Calendar

Default Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-25-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. CSR: Susan Lee, CSR #4280.

Russell Davis, Esq. appeared for and with the plaintiff Jill Toups, was placed under oath and direct examined.
Default has been entered against the Defendant.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant Christopher Ransom Jr. for Judgment as follows:

Damages - $137,000.00
Costs - $1,291.000
Emortional Distress - $1,000,000.00
Total judgment in the amount of $1,138,291.00
Counsel to prepare Judgment for signature.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PETER SPATARO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Russell M. Davis, Esq. Law Offices of Russell M. Davis, (415) 409-5627 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Lisa Marie Black, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith (415) 421-3400 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: MOTION DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT WAS A BONA-FIDE ENCUMBRANCES. E-MAILS INDICATE AMY GREEN, DEFENDANT'S ESCROW AGENT, KNEW ABOUT PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF'S POSSIBLE INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY, AND THE PRIOR LITIGATION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND RANSOM. JUDGMENT ON SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT WEBSITE INDICATED RANSOM NO LONGER HAD AN INTEREST IN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED REGARDING DEFENDANT'S IMPUTED KNOWLEDGE FROM HIS ESCROW AGENT. (SEE TRIPLE A MANAGEMENT CO., INC. V. FRISONE (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 520.) SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED DOMINION OVER HER EQUITY "DAMAGED IN AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO THE VALUE OF THE EQUITY APPROPRIATED," NOT TITLE, SO IT IS A CLAIM FOR PERSONAL PROPERTY. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED REGARDING INJUNCTION CAUSE OF ACTION WHERE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS SUPPORT AN INJUNCTION. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED REGARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT RISE TO MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Ord. Re: Objec. to Plft's Evid., etc. filed in Open Crt. and copies given to counsel at hearing. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Oct-16-2009 Continued To Oct-30-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PETER SPATARO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Russell Davis, Esq. of Attorney at Law, (415) 709-5627 appearing for Plaintiff; Lisa Marie Black, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (415) 421-3400 appearing for Defendant. (The tentative ruling is not being contested counsel only request clarification.) Hearing held. Ruling: the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Hearing continued to October 30, 2009. Plaintiff's supplemental opposition due October 22, 2009. Defendant's supplemental reply due October 27, 2009. Also, if counsel need more time to brief the issues counsel may present that request in a written and filed declaration to the Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
